summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting where Dries Homes presented a major mixed-use development concept called Eagle's Nest on the 164-acre former golf course property, featuring a big box anchor store, 300 apartments, townhomes, and single-family homes. The board approved a 12-month temporary zoning moratorium on large-scale residential developments to address zoning code concerns, authorized the purchase of a new ambulance for $368,326, and approved several other items including an SRO contract with local schools and junk vehicle resolutions for a chronic nuisance property on Linton Road. A zoning workshop was scheduled for May 22, 2025 to begin updating the township's zoning resolution.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda – passed unanimously
Motion to approve payment of bills (April 9–22, 2025) – passed unanimously
Motion to authorize Chief Myers to purchase F-550 Lifeline ambulance for $368,326 with 36-month delivery – passed unanimously
Motion to approve Resolution 006-2025 temporary 12-month zoning moratorium on large-scale residential developments (emergency) – passed unanimously
Motion to schedule Board of Trustees special meeting/zoning workshop for May 22, 2025 at 6 PM – passed unanimously
Motion to nominate Alan Freeman and Tom Peck to District 10 Public Works Integrating Committee – passed unanimously
Motion to partner with Clermont County Park District to host trick-or-treat at Sagamore Park – passed unanimously
Motion to approve Memorial Day parade on May 26 at 10 AM with Steve Endicott as Grand Marshal and Troy Day as guest speaker – passed unanimously
Motion to approve SRO contract with Goshen Local Schools for 2025-2026 school year at $153,000 – passed unanimously
Motion to approve $700 table sponsorship at annual law enforcement awards banquet – passed unanimously
Motion to approve Resolution 007-2025 determining junk motor vehicles at 6833 Linton Road – passed unanimously
Motion to approve Resolution 008-2025 determining junk motor vehicles at 6834 Linton Road – passed unanimously
Motion to adjourn into executive session at 7:12 PM per ORC 121.22(G)(1) – passed unanimously
Motion to approve Department of Development zoning grant application for a land use plan – passed unanimously
Motion to adjourn at 7:48 PM – passed unanimously

attendees: Matt Maines (Dries Homes Land Development Manager)
Kevin Mayloff (attorney, referenced for future workshop)
notes: The moratorium passed unanimously as an emergency but there was significant discussion about how it might affect the Eagle's Nest mixed-use development that was just presented. Trustees expressed concern about not wanting to miss the development opportunity while also needing to address zoning code issues. Legal counsel will need to determine whether a mixed-use PUD falls under the moratorium's scope. The junk vehicle issue at Linton Road has been ongoing since 2019 with multiple prior resolutions that were never enforced. Executive session was held regarding employment/compensation matters but no action was taken publicly. Trustee Hauserman initially expressed hesitation on the moratorium vote but ultimately voted yes.
title: BOT 4-22-25
